summary.text: A builder for creating instances of
for testing purposes.
Builds and returns a new
instance. This can only be called once.
Assembly of the app host project.
Directory of the project where the app host is located. Defaults to the content root if there's no project.
Gets the set of key/value configuration properties.

remarks.text: This can be mutated by adding more configuration sources, which will update its current view.

summary.text: Gets the information about the hosting environment an application is running in.
Eventing infrastructure for AppHost lifecycle.
Execution context for this invocation of the AppHost.

children.text: property provides access key information about the context in which the distributed application is running. The most important properties that the
properties. Developers building Aspire based applications may whish to change the application model depending on whether they are running locally, or whether they are publishing to the cloud.
. In this case an extension method is used to generate a stable node name for RabbitMQ for local development runs.

summary.text: Gets the service for managing Aspire file system operations.

children.text: provides a centralized way to manage temporary files and directories used by Aspire, enabling testability and consistent temp file management.
Resources and infrastructure code should use this service instead of static methods like
to ensure consistent directory management across the application.

summary.text: Gets the deployment pipeline for this distributed application.

remarks.text: The pipeline allows adding custom deployment steps that execute during the deploy process. Steps can declare dependencies on other steps to control execution order.

summary.text: Gets the collection of resources for the distributed application.

remarks.text: This can be mutated by adding more resources, which will update its current view.

summary.text: Gets a collection of services for the application to compose. This is useful for adding user provided or framework provided services.
Gets the service for managing user secrets.

children.text: provides a centralized way to manage user secrets used by Aspire, enabling testability and consistent secret management.
